behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the real STT model’s diarization/labeling quality, not just local wiring. A mock STT response would hide label-loss failures. System was considered, but behavior text requires correctness of model output content ("preserves speaker labels"), so Agentic is the minimum. Workflow is rejected because this behavior targets a single model-output quality property, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run with real meeting audio through the real speech-to-text API and assert transcript segments retain correct speaker labels for known attendee utterances used by downstream attribution.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/transformation logic and can fail within one local parser/object boundary. No required real OS/network/subprocess, and no required AI/ML judgment if transcript text is provided as fixture input. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text focuses on parsing extraction itself, not cross-module handoff failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser function transcript text fixtures containing explicit assignments; assert output structure contains correct assignee and task text pairs.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM output decisions about coverage/completeness of action items per attendee. Deterministic stubs would mask omission failures. Integration is rejected because behavior text evaluates live summary content quality, not only routing/retry/payload wiring. Workflow was considered but rejected: this assertion is on one summarization decision boundary, not explicitly multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Send a transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the real summarization LLM and assert output includes at least one action item for each assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic routing logic after decisions are already identified. Real infra and real model output are not required to catch misrouting. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text is about schema placement logic ("routes ... into the `decisions` section schema") rather than inter-module state propagation under real boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Given pre-identified decision records as input to routing logic, assert they are emitted only in `decisions` schema fields and not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This is LLM output quality/placement behavior: whether the model surfaces key decisions in the right section. Mocks would always pass with canned outputs and hide true failures. Integration is rejected because behavior text requires correctness of live summary content, not just deterministic post-processing. Workflow is rejected because one model judgment boundary is sufficient for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real transcript examples with explicit decisions, run real summarization call, and assert key decisions are present in `decisions` section and absent from narrative-only placement.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires sequential multi-step processing (split -> process segments -> merge) and final coherence, which depends on chained model-driven outputs across steps. Single-step Agentic is too low because inter-step handoff/merge regressions appear only across the sequence. Integration is rejected by MULTI-STEP and LLM-DEP criteria.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute recording through end-to-end segmented mode, verify segment order/coverage and merged final summary coherence with no dropped or duplicated segment content.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with real audio, real transcription model, real summarization model, and final delivery. Failures emerge at stage boundaries and agentic decisions across steps. Lower levels cannot expose full no-intervention pipeline breakages. Rejected Agentic because behavior text explicitly covers multiple sequential stages, not a single model step.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like pipeline on a real meeting recording: upload, real STT, real LLM summarization, and output delivery; assert successful completion and valid structured output without operator intervention.
